nl_statement: If f is strongly convex on s with parameter m and has gradient f'(x) at every x ∈ s, then ⟨f'(x) - f'(y), x - y⟩ ≥ m·‖x - y‖² for all x, y ∈ s.
lean_signature: theorem strong_convex_gradient_lower_bound {E : Type*} [NormedAddCommGroup E] [InnerProductSpace ℝ E] [CompleteSpace E] {s : Set E} {f : E → ℝ} {m : ℝ} {f' : E → E} (hsc : StrongConvexOn s m f) (hf : ∀ x ∈ s, HasGradientAt f (f' x) x) : ∀ x ∈ s, ∀ y ∈ s, inner ℝ (f' x - f' y) (x - y) ≥ m * ‖x - y‖ ^ 2
assumptions: f has gradient f' on s
reuse_reason: core property used by every strong convexity convergence proof
